{"key_event":"FARC peace accord and expanding civic mobilization","reasoning":"FARC’s military decline, disarmament, and transitional-justice institution-building raised the state’s coercive and legal reach, but illegal economies, armed successors, fiscal strain, and Venezuelan migration kept territorial authority uneven. Civil society became more effective through victims’, rural, Indigenous, student, environmental, media, judicial, and electoral checks, though intimidation of activists and polarization limited the gain.","society_change":0.8,"society_power":6.3,"state_change":0.4,"state_power":7.2}
